When composing latent memories, the composer must fit representations within the budget (e.g., 'max 512 tokens for agent memory'). Implement a lossy compression strategy that prioritizes high-salience information when budget is exceeded.
ideas.what_it_adds: MemoryJS has TTL and promotion mechanisms but no hard token budget constraints. This adds explicit token efficiency guarantees, critical for long-running multi-agent systems where context window is limited.

ideas.idea: Trajectory Similarity-Based Retrieval for Composition
ideas.description: Implement a retrieval mechanism that finds similar past trajectories before composing new latent memories. Use embedding-based similarity (already in MemoryJS) to fetch relevant trajectory segments, then condition the LatentMemoryComposer on both the current context and retrieved similar experiences. Add a RetrievalAugmentedComposer class.
ideas.what_it_adds: MemoryJS has semantic search but doesn't use retrieved trajectories to condition memory composition. This adds in-context learning: agents compose memories informed by similar past experiences, improving memory relevance.

ideas.idea: Differentiable Memory Reconstruction
ideas.description: Make latent memory vectors differentiable end-to-end: implement the LatentMemoryComposer as a neural module (e.g., transformer encoder) that can receive gradients from downstream task losses. Store computation graphs so LMPO can backpropagate through memory composition decisions. Add a GradientTracker for debugging.
ideas.what_it_adds: MemoryJS memories are static after creation. This adds gradient flow through memory, enabling automatic optimization of what gets remembered based on task outcomes.

ideas.idea: Memory Homogenization Detection & Alerting
ideas.description: Add a MemoryDiversityMonitor that detects when multiple agents' latent memories become too similar (cosine similarity > threshold). Trigger alerts and suggest role profile adjustments. Implement a diversity loss that can be added to LMPO to encourage role-specific memory divergence.
ideas.what_it_adds: MemoryJS has no mechanism to detect or prevent memory homogenization across agents. This adds observability and automatic correction for the key problem LatentMem solves.

ideas.idea: Cross-Agent Memory Transfer Learning
ideas.description: Implement a MemoryTransferModule that allows learned role profiles and latent memory composers from one MAS instance to initialize another. Add fine-tuning capabilities so profiles can adapt to new task domains. Support profile merging for heterogeneous agent teams.
ideas.what_it_adds: MemoryJS has import/export but no transfer learning for learned memory behaviors. This adds the ability to reuse learned memory strategies across different multi-agent systems.

paper: MemMamba — Rethinking Memory Patterns in State Space Model
innovation: MemMamba introduces a 'note-taking' mechanism that combines state summarization with cross-layer/cross-token attention to preserve salient information in long sequences while maintaining linear complexity, solving the exponential memory decay problem in state-space models.

ideas.idea: Horizontal-Vertical Memory Fidelity Metrics
ideas.what_it_adds: Quantitative framework to measure information degradation both within layers (horizontal) and across layers (vertical). Currently MemoryJS has decay engines and salience engines but no formal fidelity metrics to diagnose WHERE and HOW information is being lost in the knowledge graph.
ideas.implementation: Add fidelity_score calculation to observation/relation tracking; measure semantic transmission loss at each graph depth level; expose metrics in search/retrieval diagnostics

ideas.idea: State Summarization for Long Observation Sequences
ideas.what_it_adds: Automatic distillation of verbose observation chains into compressed summaries (like 'note-taking'), preserving salient facts while reducing storage/retrieval overhead. MemoryJS has consolidation pipeline but not intelligent summarization that maintains semantic fidelity across long episodic sequences.
ideas.implementation: Add summarization layer to episodic memory; compress observation chains >N tokens into salient fact summaries; maintain bidirectional links to original observations; apply during consolidation pipeline

ideas.idea: Cross-Layer Attention Mechanism for Multi-Depth Queries
ideas.what_it_adds: Enable queries to simultaneously attend to entities/relations at different 'depths' in the knowledge graph hierarchy, not just sequential traversal. Current graph algorithms (Dijkstra, PageRank) don't support attention-weighted cross-depth information fusion.
ideas.implementation: Implement cross-layer attention weights in graph traversal; allow semantic search to weight results by depth relevance; add 'depth-aware' ranking to hybrid search combining BM25 + cross-layer attention scores

ideas.idea: Exponential Decay Analysis for Relation Chains
ideas.what_it_adds: Formal mathematical model of how information degrades through multi-hop relation chains (similar to MemMamba's exponential decay analysis). MemoryJS has decay engine but no analytical framework to predict/measure information loss through N-hop paths.
ideas.implementation: Add decay coefficient calculation for relation chains; expose decay curves in graph analytics; warn when queries traverse paths with >threshold decay; suggest alternative shorter paths

ideas.idea: Salient Information Extraction & Pinning
ideas.what_it_adds: Automatic identification and 'pinning' of high-salience facts to prevent decay (inspired by MemMamba's salient information preservation). Current salience engine ranks but doesn't actively protect critical facts from TTL/decay.
ideas.implementation: Add 'pin' flag to observations/relations; exempt pinned items from decay; auto-pin based on salience score + query frequency; expose pinning in consolidation pipeline

ideas.idea: Cross-Session Attention for Multi-Agent Memory
ideas.what_it_adds: Enable agents to attend to relevant facts from other agents' sessions (not just visibility filtering). MemoryJS has multi-agent with visibility levels but no mechanism to weight/attend across private/shared memories based on relevance.
ideas.implementation: Add cross-session semantic attention in multi-agent queries; weight shared/public observations by relevance to current agent's context; expose in agent memory search

ideas.idea: Information Bottleneck Detection
ideas.what_it_adds: Diagnostic tool to identify where critical information is being lost in the knowledge graph (e.g., bottleneck relations, high-decay paths). MemoryJS has pattern detector but not bottleneck analysis.
ideas.implementation: Add bottleneck detection to pattern detector; identify relations with high information loss; suggest graph restructuring; expose in compression/archiving recommendations

paper: MemGovern — Enhancing Code Agents through Learning from Governed Human Experiences
innovation: MemGovern transforms unstructured cross-repository GitHub data into standardized 'experience cards' through multi-stage governance (curation, purification, dual-layer protocol), enabling agents to retrieve and apply human debugging patterns rather than relying on shallow semantic matching.

ideas.idea: Experience Card Schema with Standardized Dimensions
ideas.what_it_adds: A structured template for memory entries capturing: problem signature, modification strategy, validation method, cross-repo applicability score, and repair logic (not just semantic content). Currently MemoryJS stores observations/relations but lacks domain-specific card schemas with repair-pattern metadata.

ideas.idea: Multi-Stage Data Governance Pipeline
ideas.what_it_adds: Automated curation layer that: (1) filters noise from raw data sources, (2) purifies heterogeneous content (terminology normalization, style standardization), (3) validates card quality before indexing. MemoryJS has CRUD but no pre-ingestion governance or quality gates.

ideas.idea: Dual-Layer Retrieval Protocol
ideas.what_it_adds: Separate retrieval signals (semantic matching, metadata filters) from actionable repair logic (step-by-step fix patterns). Current hybrid search combines signals but doesn't distinguish between 'finding relevant cases' and 'extracting executable reasoning'. Enables logic-driven rather than similarity-driven retrieval.

ideas.idea: Agentic Multi-Round Search & Browse
ideas.what_it_adds: Interactive search loop where agents iteratively refine queries based on retrieved results (not one-shot retrieval). Mirrors human exploration of prior cases. MemoryJS search is stateless; this adds session-aware, iterative refinement with context accumulation.

ideas.idea: Cross-Repository Applicability Scoring
ideas.what_it_adds: Metadata field quantifying how transferable a repair pattern is across projects (accounting for terminology variance, module organization, coding style differences). MemoryJS has no mechanism to score generalizability of stored experiences.

ideas.idea: Repair Pattern Extraction & Normalization
ideas.what_it_adds: Automated extraction of modification strategies (e.g., 'null-check insertion', 'type-cast addition') and normalization across syntax variations. MemoryJS pattern detector identifies frequency patterns but not repair-specific transformations.

ideas.idea: Validation Method Tracking
ideas.what_it_adds: Store how each repair was validated (test suite, manual verification, CI/CD) as part of experience cards. Enables agents to assess confidence in retrieved solutions. MemoryJS has no validation provenance metadata.

ideas.idea: Noise Filtering & Content Purification Operators
ideas.what_it_adds: Configurable filters to remove social exchanges, procedural noise, and non-technical content from raw sources before graph ingestion. MemoryJS accepts all observations; this adds pre-processing rules for domain-specific noise.

paper: Conditional Memory via Scalable Lookup — A New Axis of Sparsity for Large Language Models
innovation: Engram introduces O(1) deterministic N-gram embedding lookup as a complementary sparsity axis to MoE, separating static knowledge retrieval from dynamic computation. This reveals a U-shaped scaling law optimizing the trade-off between neural computation and static memory allocation.

ideas.idea: Deterministic N-gram Hashing Layer for Entity/Pattern Lookup
ideas.what_it_adds: Add a hashed N-gram embedding table (2-gram, 3-gram, etc.) that enables O(1) constant-time retrieval of frequently co-occurring entity patterns and formulaic sequences. Current memoryjs uses semantic embeddings + BM25; this adds deterministic, collision-resistant hashing for stereotyped local dependencies without neural computation.

ideas.idea: Sparsity Allocation Optimizer (Compute vs Memory Trade-off)
ideas.what_it_adds: Implement a U-shaped scaling law analyzer that profiles the optimal parameter budget split between dynamic computation (reasoning depth) and static memory (lookup tables). MemoryJS currently has no mechanism to measure or optimize this trade-off; this would auto-tune which entity types/patterns should be stored vs computed.

ideas.idea: Context-Aware Gating for Memory Fusion
ideas.what_it_adds: Add a learned gating mechanism that dynamically modulates retrieved static embeddings based on current context (hidden state). This allows the graph to decide when to trust lookup results vs. recompute based on reasoning depth needed—memoryjs currently has no fusion layer between retrieved facts and reasoning context.

ideas.idea: Infrastructure-Aware Prefetching for Host Memory Offloading
ideas.what_it_adds: Implement deterministic addressing + runtime prefetching to offload large embedding tables to host memory with <3% overhead. MemoryJS currently stores in SQLite/JSONL but has no prefetch optimization; this enables scaling to 100B+ parameter lookup tables without GPU memory constraints.

ideas.idea: Multi-Head Hashing for Collision Resolution
ideas.what_it_adds: Extend the hashing layer with multiple independent hash functions (multi-head hashing) to reduce collisions in N-gram lookups. Current memoryjs has no hash-based retrieval; this adds robustness for high-cardinality entity patterns.

ideas.idea: Mechanistic Analysis Layer (Logit Lens / CKA Integration)
ideas.what_it_adds: Add introspection tools to measure which layers are performing static reconstruction vs. reasoning, and which attention heads focus on local vs. global context. MemoryJS has no layer-wise analysis; this reveals whether memory lookups are actually freeing up capacity for deeper reasoning.

ideas.idea: Long-Context Attention Capacity Analyzer
ideas.what_it_adds: Track how delegating local dependencies to lookups frees attention for global context. Measure long-context retrieval performance (NIAH, variable tracking) separately from short-context. MemoryJS has context window management but no metric for attention capacity liberation.

ideas.idea: Tokenizer-Compressed N-gram Indexing
ideas.what_it_adds: Compress multi-token entities (e.g., 'Alexander the Great') into single hash keys via tokenizer-aware compression before lookup. MemoryJS stores entities as-is; this reduces embedding table size while maintaining retrieval accuracy for multi-token patterns.
